title: Ensure session Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less

input_row.title: Ensure session Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less
input_row.description: Idle disconnect timeout in minutes is the amount of time that users can be inactive before they are disconnected from their streaming session and the Disconnect timeout in minutes time begins.
input_row.rationale: Users are considered idle when they stop providing keyboard or mouse input during their streaming session. File uploads and downloads, audio in, audio out, and pixels changing do not qualify as user activity. Once disconnected from their streaming session the Disconnect timeout begins.
input_row.audit: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and confirm that Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less. If Idle disconnect timeout is set to anything greater than 10 minutes refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and click Edit 5. Change the Idle disconnect timeout to 5 minutes or less and click Save changes

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_appstream2_fleet_idle_disconnect_timeout_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- Utilize the AWS SDK (boto3) to check the idle disconnect timeout configuration for AppStream 2.0 fleets.
- Use the following to enumerate fleets:
  - `boto3.client('appstream').describe_fleets()` to list all fleets and retrieve their configurations.
- Check the `IdleDisconnectTimeout` attribute of each fleet configuration.
- Define clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: `IdleDisconnectTimeout` is less than or equal to 10.
  - Fail: `IdleDisconnectTimeout` is greater than 10.
- Example:
  ```python
  import boto3

  appstream = boto3.client('appstream')
  response = appstream.describe_fleets()
  
  for fleet in response['Fleets']:
      idle_timeout = fleet.get('IdleDisconnectTimeout')
      if idle_timeout is not None and idle_timeout > 10:
          print(f'Fleet {fleet["Name"]}: Fail - IdleDisconnectTimeout is {idle_timeout}')
      else:
          print(f'Fleet {fleet["Name"]}: Pass - IdleDisconnectTimeout is {idle_timeout}')
  ```
